intro: Costularia xyridioides, commonly known as Costularia, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Costularia xyridioides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Costularia xyridioides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, colds, and skin infections.
